title: She’s running Boston for the hospital that saved her daughter’s life
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Leslie Wallus is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital's pediatric oncology unit.
Wallus's daughter, Eloise, survived a brain tumor thanks to treatment at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Wallus's marathon participation honors Eloise's strength and supports families facing similar diagnoses.

title: She’s running Boston to support people with visual impairments
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Fransisca Miraningtyas, 46, from Jakarta, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Massachusetts Association for the Blind and Visually Impaired.
Miraningtyas aims to support those with visual impairments, inspired by her own experience with pterygium, an eye condition requiring surgery.
Her workplace has covered her eye surgery costs, highlighting the financial barriers faced by others needing similar procedures.

title: Westfield man busted in alleged Red Bull SNAP scam at Chicopee smoke shop
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mohamed Yahiya, 47, accused of fraudulent SNAP transactions at Chicopee smoke shop.
Undercover investigator bought $1,731.34 in Red Bull, received $1,046 cash back.
Yahiya faces multiple charges including food stamp trafficking and possessing unstamped cigarettes.

title: Free speech rally planned outside BU president’s office amid Pride flag dispute
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Free speech rally planned outside BU President Melissa Gilliam's office after Pride flags removed due to signage policy.
Terrier Courage, the rally organizer, has gathered over 1,800 signatures for a petition against the flag policy.
The petition seeks policy revisions to allow signage in offices and dorm rooms, supporting free expression on campus.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in April
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: April 2026 sees new releases on Netflix, Hulu, HBO Max, Disney+, Prime Video, Apple TV, Peacock, and Paramount+.
HBO Max features the final season of 'Hacks' on April 9 and 'Euphoria' Season 3 on April 13.
Netflix highlights include the Emmy-winning 'Beef' Season 2 and several Massachusetts-connected productions.

title: What to know about the MBTA’s free and discounted commuter rail plans for summer
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MBTA offers free commuter rail service on Fridays from June to August, excluding special event trains like CapeFLYER.
Monthly commuter rail passes are half-price for June to August, not applicable to Zone 1A or single tickets.
Weekend rail trips include $1 companion fare; excludes special event trains to Foxborough requiring regular fare.

title: Weston native Tim Demirjian wins Hulu’s ‘Love Overboard’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Weston native Tim Demirjian won Hulu's 'Love Overboard' with Gia Aldisert, earning a trip around the world.
Demirjian was cast for the show after receiving a random text from a casting director while at a music festival.
The show features 16 singles on a yacht in Malta, with couples enjoying luxury and singles doing chores below deck.

title: MBTA to release train tickets for World Cup matches at Boston Stadium
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA train tickets for World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium will be on sale starting April 8 through the mTicket app.
Fans need a same-day World Cup match ticket to purchase train tickets, which are nonrefundable and non-transferable.
Enhanced security measures at Gillette include specific bag policies; prohibited items are also banned from trains.

title: 2 Boston bars earn spots on 50 Best’s top 100 U.S. bars list
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: The Wig Shop, at 27 Temple Place, is ranked 86th on the 50 Best U.S. bars list.
Hecate, found at 48 Gloucester St., claims the 88th spot for its mystical-themed cocktails.
Both Boston bars, new entries on the list, specialize in craft cocktails and opened in 2022.

title: Mass. man sentenced for online threats to public figures, children, elementary school
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Funwayo Mbilini Nyawo, 37, received 32 months in prison for online threats to public figures and children.
Nyawo pleaded guilty to 13 counts of interstate threatening communications and one count of stalking.
Posts from July to October 2024 targeted police, children, and officials with violent threats and graphic images.
